component.name: Quick Web Apps
component.summary: Turn any website into a desktop app

description.p: Quick Web Apps is a native GTK4 and libadwaita manager for the GNOME desktop that turns websites into first-class applications. Paste a URL and it detects the site's Web App Manifest, fills in the name and icon for you, and installs a launcher into your app grid.
Each web app runs in its own isolated window with its own profile and dock identity. Features include:

ul.li: PWA manifest auto-detection with automatic name, icon and theme
Automatic icon download with a clean lettered fallback
URL-scope confinement so off-scope links open in your browser
Per-app user agent, ad-blocking, zoom and custom CSS

description.p: Fixes a data-loss bug: deleting a web app no longer removes a custom icon you picked from elsewhere on disk. Imported icons are now copied into the app's own icon folder, and deleting an app only ever removes files it owns.

Web content is now crisp under fractional and HiDPI scaling (the render path is scale-aware, fixing blurry pages on compositors like Niri). Right-click inside a web app now shows a context menu (back/forward/reload and cut/copy/paste/select all), and the manager gains an About window so you can see which version you're running.

Deeper desktop integration: media apps now expose MPRIS controls (lock screen, media keys, Quick Settings); mail/chat apps can show an unread count badge on the dock; and background apps get a tray icon (show/quit) plus an optional "Start on login".

Apps that share a login profile can now run at the same time, in one process with one shared session. New "Identify as" option spoofs the browser OS (Windows/macOS/Mobile/Custom) for services that require it. Clearer messaging when a profile is already in use.

Camera and microphone now work for video calls (Teams, Zoom, Meet) and are allowed by default. Adds a portable AppImage build for any distribution. Fixes blank toggle labels and makes the link-handling options readable.

A big round of per-app controls and fixes: built-in ad/tracker blocker, force light/dark appearance, custom CSS, background-app mode (keep running for notifications), downloads to your Downloads folder, and a colored login-profile indicator in the manager and window. Permission policy for camera/mic and location, crisper rendering under fractional/HiDPI scaling, more reliable launcher paths, and groundwork for safe config migrations.

Media apps now have audio in the Flatpak sandbox. New tri-state link handling (keep everything in-window, or send other sites to the browser by registrable domain or exact host), with an allowlist that keeps sign-in/SSO and CAPTCHA pages in-window. Windows remember their size and zoom between sessions. Cleaner handling of declaratively-managed default-app settings.

Updated to the GNOME 50 runtime (47 and 48 are end-of-life). Apps with multi-domain sign-in (Microsoft Outlook/Teams, Slack) now keep authentication in-window by default, fixing broken logins. Link handling is more reliable.

Embedded service panels (Gmail's Tasks, Keep and Calendar) now load in-window instead of hanging or spawning stray windows. Mail and chat apps open links from messages in the system browser by default, while multi-domain sign-in still completes inside the app.
Initial development release: create web apps with manifest autofill, automatic icons, and app-grid launcher install.
